mnemonics: Every Transaction Is Very Important For Blockchain Ledger (ETIVIFBL) - representing the components: Version, Inputs, Outputs, lockTime
keyPoints: A transaction is a data structure that encodes transfer of value between participants
Transactions contain inputs (which reference previous outputs) and outputs (which specify new ownership)
Each transaction is cryptographically signed to prove ownership of the inputs being spent
commonMistakes: Confusing transaction inputs with outputs (inputs spend previous outputs, outputs create new spendable amounts)
Assuming transactions directly contain sender/receiver addresses (they reference and create outputs with locking scripts)
Forgetting that transactions must be validated against the entire blockchain history to prevent double-spending
